root_text.an10.14:1.1: “Yassa kassaci, bhikkhave, bhikkhussa vā bhikkhuniyā vā pañca cetokhilā appahīnā pañca cetasovinibandhā asamucchinnā, tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati hāniyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu no vuddhi.
root_text.an10.14:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satthari kaṅkhati vicikicchati nādhimuccati na sampasīdati.
root_text.an10.14:2.3: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satthari kaṅkhati vicikicchati nādhimuccati na sampasīdati, tassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an10.14:2.4: Yassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, evamassāyaṁ paṭhamo cetokhilo appahīno hoti.
root_text.an10.14:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhamme kaṅkhati …pe…
root_text.an10.14:3.4: sabrahmacārīsu kupito hoti anattamano āhatacitto khilajāto.
root_text.an10.14:3.5: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sabrahmacārīsu kupito hoti anattamano āhatacitto khilajāto, tassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an10.14:3.6: Yassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, evamassāyaṁ pañcamo cetokhilo appahīno hoti.
root_text.an10.14:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāmesu avītarāgo hoti avigatacchando avigatapemo avigatapipāso avigatapariḷāho avigatataṇho.
root_text.an10.14:4.3: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāmesu avītarāgo hoti avigatacchando avigatapemo avigatapipāso avigatapariḷāho avigatataṇho, tassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an10.14:4.4: Yassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, evamassāyaṁ paṭhamo cetasovinibandho asamucchinno hoti.
root_text.an10.14:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye avītarāgo hoti …pe…
root_text.an10.14:5.3: yāvadatthaṁ udarāvadehakaṁ bhuñjitvā seyyasukhaṁ passasukhaṁ middhasukhaṁ anuyutto viharati …
root_text.an10.14:5.4: aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ paṇidhāya brahmacariyaṁ carati: ‘imināhaṁ sīlena vā vatena vā tapena vā brahmacariyena vā devo vā bhavissāmi devaññataro vā’ti.
root_text.an10.14:5.5: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ paṇidhāya brahmacariyaṁ carati: ‘imināhaṁ sīlena vā vatena vā tapena vā brahmacariyena vā devo vā bhavissāmi devaññataro vā’ti, tassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an10.14:5.6: Yassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, evamassāyaṁ pañcamo cetasovinibandho asamucchinno hoti.
root_text.an10.14:6.1: Yassa kassaci, bhikkhave, bhikkhussa vā bhikkhuniyā vā ime pañca cetokhilā appahīnā ime pañca cetasovinibandhā asamucchinnā, tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati hāniyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu no vuddhi.
root_text.an10.14:7.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, kāḷapakkhe candassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, hāyateva vaṇṇena hāyati maṇḍalena hāyati ābhāya hāyati ārohapariṇāhena;
root_text.an10.14:7.2: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, yassa kassaci bhikkhussa vā bhikkhuniyā vā ime pañca cetokhilā appahīnā ime pañca cetasovinibandhā asamucchinnā, tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati hāniyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu no vuddhi.
root_text.an10.14:8.1: Yassa kassaci, bhikkhave, bhikkhussa vā bhikkhuniyā vā pañca cetokhilā pahīnā pañca cetasovinibandhā susamucchinnā, tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati vuddhiyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu no parihāni.
root_text.an10.14:9.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satthari na kaṅkhati na vicikicchati, adhimuccati sampasīdati.
root_text.an10.14:9.3: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satthari na kaṅkhati na vicikicchati adhimuccati sampasīdati, tassa cittaṁ namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an10.14:9.4: Yassa cittaṁ namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, evamassāyaṁ paṭhamo cetokhilo pahīno hoti.
root_text.an10.14:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhamme na kaṅkhati …pe…
root_text.an10.14:10.4: sabrahmacārīsu na kupito hoti attamano na āhatacitto na khilajāto.
root_text.an10.14:10.5: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sabrahmacārīsu na kupito hoti attamano na āhatacitto na khilajāto, tassa cittaṁ namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an10.14:10.6: Yassa cittaṁ namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, evamassāyaṁ pañcamo cetokhilo pahīno hoti.
root_text.an10.14:11.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāmesu vītarāgo hoti vigatacchando vigatapemo vigatapipāso vigatapariḷāho vigatataṇho.
root_text.an10.14:11.3: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāmesu vītarāgo hoti vigatacchando vigatapemo vigatapipāso vigatapariḷāho vigatataṇho, tassa cittaṁ namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an10.14:11.4: Yassa cittaṁ namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, evamassāyaṁ paṭhamo cetasovinibandho susamucchinno hoti.
root_text.an10.14:12.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye vītarāgo hoti …pe…
root_text.an10.14:12.3: na yāvadatthaṁ udarāvadehakaṁ bhuñjitvā seyyasukhaṁ passasukhaṁ middhasukhaṁ anuyutto viharati,
root_text.an10.14:12.4: na aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ paṇidhāya brahmacariyaṁ carati: ‘imināhaṁ sīlena vā vatena vā tapena vā brahmacariyena vā devo vā bhavissāmi devaññataro vā’ti.
root_text.an10.14:12.5: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ paṇidhāya …pe… devaññataro vāti, tassa cittaṁ namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an10.14:12.6: Yassa cittaṁ namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, evamassāyaṁ pañcamo cetasovinibandho susamucchinno hoti.
root_text.an10.14:13.1: Yassa kassaci, bhikkhave, bhikkhussa vā bhikkhuniyā vā ime pañca cetokhilā pahīnā ime pañca cetasovinibandhā susamucchinnā, tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati vuddhiyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu no parihāni.
root_text.an10.14:14.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, juṇhapakkhe candassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, vaḍḍhateva vaṇṇena vaḍḍhati maṇḍalena vaḍḍhati ābhāya vaḍḍhati ārohapariṇāhena;
root_text.an10.14:14.2: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, yassa kassaci bhikkhussa vā bhikkhuniyā vā ime pañca cetokhilā pahīnā ime pañca cetasovinibandhā susamucchinnā, tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati vuddhiyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu no parihānī”ti.

translation_text.an10.14:1.1: “Mendicants, a monk or nun who has not given up five kinds of hard-heartedness and has not severed five shackles of the heart can expect decline, not growth, in skillful qualities, whether by day or by night.
translation_text.an10.14:2.1: What are the five kinds of hard-heartedness they haven’t given up?
translation_text.an10.14:2.2: Firstly, a mendicant has doubts about the Teacher. They’re uncertain, undecided, and lacking confidence.
translation_text.an10.14:2.3: This being so, their mind doesn’t incline toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an10.14:2.4: This is the first kind of hard-heartedness they haven’t given up.
translation_text.an10.14:3.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has doubts about the teaching …
translation_text.an10.14:3.4: A mendicant is angry and upset with their spiritual companions, resentful and closed off.
translation_text.an10.14:3.5: This being so, their mind doesn’t incline toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an10.14:3.6: This is the fifth kind of hard-heartedness they haven’t given up.
translation_text.an10.14:3.7: These are the five kinds of hard-heartedness they haven’t given up.
translation_text.an10.14:4.1: What are the five shackles of the heart they haven’t severed?
translation_text.an10.14:4.2: Firstly, a mendicant isn’t free of greed, desire, fondness, thirst, passion, and craving for sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an10.14:4.3: This being so, their mind doesn’t incline toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an10.14:4.4: This is the first shackle of the heart they haven’t severed.
translation_text.an10.14:5.1: Furthermore, a mendicant isn’t free of greed for the body …
translation_text.an10.14:5.2: They’re not free of greed for form …
translation_text.an10.14:5.3: They eat as much as they like until their belly is full, then indulge in the pleasures of sleeping, lying down, and drowsing …
translation_text.an10.14:5.4: They lead the spiritual life wishing to be reborn in one of the orders of gods: ‘By this precept or observance or fervent austerity or spiritual practice, may I become one of the gods!’
translation_text.an10.14:5.5: This being so, their mind doesn’t incline toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an10.14:5.6: This is the fifth shackle of the heart they haven’t severed.
translation_text.an10.14:5.7: These are the five shackles of the heart they haven’t severed.
translation_text.an10.14:6.1: A monk or nun who has not given up these five kinds of hard-heartedness and has not severed these five shackles of the heart can expect decline, not growth, in skillful qualities, whether by day or by night.
translation_text.an10.14:7.1: It’s like the moon in the waning fortnight. Whether by day or by night, its beauty, roundness, luminosity, and diameter and circumference only decline.
translation_text.an10.14:7.2: In the same way, monk or nun who has not given up these five kinds of hard-heartedness and has not severed these five shackles of the heart can expect decline, not growth, in skillful qualities, whether by day or by night.
translation_text.an10.14:8.1: A monk or nun who has given up five kinds of hard-heartedness and has severed five shackles of the heart can expect growth, not decline, in skillful qualities, whether by day or by night.
translation_text.an10.14:9.1: What are the five kinds of hard-heartedness they’ve given up?
translation_text.an10.14:9.2: Firstly, a mendicant has no doubts about the Teacher. They’re not uncertain, undecided, or lacking confidence.
translation_text.an10.14:9.3: This being so, their mind inclines toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an10.14:9.4: This is the first kind of hard-heartedness they’ve given up.
translation_text.an10.14:10.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has no doubts about the teaching …
translation_text.an10.14:10.4: A mendicant is not angry and upset with their spiritual companions, not resentful or closed off.
translation_text.an10.14:10.5: This being so, their mind inclines toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an10.14:10.6: This is the fifth kind of hard-heartedness they’ve given up.
translation_text.an10.14:10.7: These are the five kinds of hard-heartedness they’ve given up.
translation_text.an10.14:11.1: What are the five shackles of the heart they’ve severed?
translation_text.an10.14:11.2: Firstly, a mendicant is rid of greed, desire, fondness, thirst, passion, and craving for sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an10.14:11.3: This being so, their mind inclines toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an10.14:11.4: This is the first shackle of the heart they’ve severed.
translation_text.an10.14:12.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is rid of greed for the body …
translation_text.an10.14:12.2: They’re rid of greed for form …
translation_text.an10.14:12.3: They don’t eat as much as they like until their belly is full, then indulge in the pleasures of sleeping, lying down, and drowsing …
translation_text.an10.14:12.4: They don’t lead the spiritual life wishing to be reborn in one of the orders of gods: ‘By this precept or observance or fervent austerity or spiritual practice, may I become one of the gods!’
translation_text.an10.14:12.5: This being so, their mind inclines toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an10.14:12.6: This is the fifth shackle of the heart they’ve severed.
translation_text.an10.14:12.7: These are the five shackles of the heart they’ve severed.
translation_text.an10.14:13.1: A monk or nun who has given up these five kinds of hard-heartedness and has severed these five shackles of the heart can expect growth, not decline, in skillful qualities, whether by day or by night.
translation_text.an10.14:14.1: It’s like the moon in the waxing fortnight. Whether by day or by night, its beauty, roundness, luminosity, and diameter and circumference only grow.
translation_text.an10.14:14.2: In the same way, a monk or nun who has given up these five kinds of hard-heartedness and has severed these five shackles of the heart can expect growth, not decline, in skillful qualities, whether by day or by night.”
